name: Narrow prior discovery results through follow-up constraints
notes: Checks whether session-scoped carry-forward preserves candidate sets and constraints across a narrowing chain.

turns.user: Breaking out indie games this month
turns.expectation: Should establish a game candidate set with exact trend framing.
Should narrow the prior result set instead of starting a fresh unrelated ranking.

turns.user: Which of those are Steam Deck verified?
turns.expectation: Should keep the narrowed set and apply the Steam Deck constraint.

name: Resolve omitted company references from prior game context
notes: Checks whether the chat can carry a resolved game and its publisher into a follow-up comparison without the user restating names.

turns.expectation: Should resolve the game and establish active game/company context.

turns.user: How does the publisher compare to similar studios?
turns.expectation: Should resolve the omitted publisher reference and stay inside that company comparison flow.

name: Narrow similarity results without losing the reference game
notes: Checks whether follow-up constraints reuse the prior similarity result set instead of restarting broad search from scratch.

turns.user: Games like Hades with better reviews
turns.expectation: Should establish the Hades-based similarity candidate set.
Should narrow the same similarity set by review-count constraint.

turns.user: Which of those are Steam Deck verified?
turns.expectation: Should preserve both the reference game and the narrowed candidate set.

name: Drill from cross-game change discovery into single-title detail
notes: Checks whether the runtime carries change-intel context and allows a single supporting detail fetch instead of broadening again.

turns.user: What are the biggest Steam page refreshes lately?
turns.expectation: Should return a ranked change-intel set.

turns.user: Show me the recent Steam changes for Hades II
turns.expectation: Should pivot to the single-title timeline cleanly.

turns.user: Compare the latest Steam-page refresh for Hades II
turns.expectation: Should use before/after detail rather than repeating a generic timeline.

name: Recover from a sparse or no-match answer with a constrained relaxation
notes: Checks whether the follow-up remains anchored to the earlier failed screen instead of resetting the query family.

turns.user: Highly rated games under $2 released in the past year
turns.expectation: Should either return a sparse set or explain the no-match condition clearly.
Should perform one controlled relaxation of the prior screen.

name: Carry company context into a direct comparison follow-up
notes: Checks whether a company-focused follow-up can reuse the prior result set without an unnecessary new lookup chain.

turns.user: Show me all games by FromSoftware
turns.expectation: Should resolve FromSoftware and return the developer game list.
Should keep the FromSoftware game set and answer from that constrained context.
